result:
Implemented link-aware rename.

`renameNote` in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go) now renames the note file and then rewrites matching `[[old-id]]` references in every `notes/*.md` file. The rewrite is exact-target only, preserves surrounding whitespace inside links like `[[ old-id ]]`, updates self-links in the renamed note, and rolls the filename back if the reference update step fails.

I added regression coverage in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go) for cross-note reference updates, repeated links, archived notes, spacing-preserving rewrites, and backlink correctness after rename. I also updated the user-facing rename description in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md) and the CLI help text in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go).

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` in this environment because the Go toolchain is not installed: `gofmt: command not found` and `go: command not found`.
